title: Choose Instructions, Skills, Plugins, Hooks, or MCP · Design Defense
summary: Route recurring agent needs to the smallest durable extension surface. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an extension-surface decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.
Use AGENTS.md or equivalent repository guidance for durable local conventions.
Use a skill for a reusable workflow, expertise, scripts, references, or assets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

steps.title: Scope Layers · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Scope Layers without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.

content.text: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext surface choice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Scope Layers, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an extension-surface decision matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: All recurring agent behavior belongs in a skill. This fails the Scope Layers contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate one-turn prompts, repository instructions, reusable skills, enforcement hooks, plugins, and external tools by scope.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Scope Layers evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Scope Layers assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Plugin Fit applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use a plugin to distribute skills, connectors, hooks, and presentation assets as one installable unit.

hints: Name the Plugin Fit precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every personal skill requires a plugin package.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using plugin and distribute plus specific case evidence.
